rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN STATE BROADCASTING, LLC VS. 400 2ND STREET LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 18, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT ARIZONA TEMPLE HOTEL CORPORATION AND CRESLEIGH MANAGEMENT, INC.'S Motion To Set Aside Default. Defendants Arizona Temple Corporation and Cresleigh Management, Inc.'s motion to set aside the defaults entered against them on October 24, 2017 is granted. The defaults are now vacated. Moving defendants have shown that they are entitled to the relief they seek per CCP 473(b). Very slight evidence is required to support vacating defaults and doing so comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. (Shamblain v. Brattain (1988) 44 Cal. 3d 474, 478). Nor has plaintiff Golden State Broadcasting, LLC shown that it would suffer any legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS ELDER VS. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT & Notice Pursuant To Ccp 437c
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 18, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LOUIS ELDER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT & Notice Pursuant To CCP 437c. Plaintiff Louis Elder's motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion is both procedurally and substantively deficient. Mr. Elder has not alleged a breach of contract claim and relief on a motion for summary judgment may not be granted on an unalleged claim. As was stated in the April 2, 2018 order denying Mr. Elder's CCP 664.6 motion, Mr. Elder has not shown that the parties entered into a settlement. In particular, Mr. Elder failed to show that the asserted agreement complied with SF Charter 6.102, was signed by the City, approved by SFPD, which is a condition specifically set forth in the asserted agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: TIEMING YAO VS. YANYING LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 18, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANTS YANYING LIU AND QINGLING XU DEMURRER'S TO COMPLAINT. Defendants Yanying Liu and Qingling Xu's demurrer to all causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Tieming Yao is sustained as to all causes of action with 15 days leave to amend. The allegations of the complaint and the attachments are not inconsistent. Mr. Yao alleges that the individuals had an agreement and the payments were merely made through the corporations. The attachments are consistent with this theory of liability and Mr. Yao's allegations are accepted as true on this demurrer. Nonetheless, the court's copy of the complaint is missing exhibits J and K, which Mr. Yao references. Paragraph 3 of exhibit K supports Mr. Yao's position that the individuals had an agreement. Mr. Yao has leave to correct the error in failing to attach exhibits J and K. Once those documents are attached, the court does not expect to see another demurrer because the attachments are consistent with Mr. Yao's theory of liability and extrinsic evidence is necessary to determine the parties to the loans. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Yao is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)
